title: Sausage casserole
subtitle: Pork sausages braised in tomato sauce with peppers and paprika
excerpt: Pork sausages browned then simmered in a rich tomato sauce with peppers, paprika, and herbs until tender. A one-pan weeknight supper that improves if left to stand. Serve with mashed potato, rice, or crusty bread to mop up the sauce.
sourceNotes: Sausage casserole is a British weeknight staple with no single historical origin — it is the result of convenience cooking with sausages as the protein and tinned tomatoes as the base. The paprika and pepper version is the most common contemporary form. It belongs to the category of dishes that every family has a slightly different version of.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well. Cool completely, freeze in portions, and reheat gently.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe — the sauce scales without any change in method.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Makes 2 to 3 days ahead. The sauce thickens and improves on the second day.

content.text: Brown the sausages properly before adding any liquid. A pale, unbrowned sausage going into a tomato sauce produces a grey, watery result. The browned sausage skin seals in the fat and gives the sauce something to build on.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a large pan over medium-high heat. Brown the {{sausages-pork}} all over, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside. In the same pan, cook the {{onion}} and {{pepper-red}} over medium heat for 8 minutes until softened. Add the {{garlic}} and {{paprika-sweet}} and cook for 1 minute. Add the {{tomato-puree}} and stir for 1 minute. Add the {{tinned-tomatoes}} and {{bay-leaves}}. Season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Return the sausages to the pan. Simmer uncovered for 25 minutes, turning the sausages once, until the sauce has thickened and the sausages are cooked through. Remove the bay leaves. Serve with mashed potato or rice.

items.symptom: Watery sauce that does not thicken
items.cause: Too much liquid added, or not simmered long enough uncovered
items.fix: Simmer uncovered for the full 25 minutes. Tinned tomatoes vary in water content — if still thin, continue simmering for another 10 minutes

items.cause: Initial browning at too high a heat
items.fix: Brown at medium-high heat, not full heat. Once in the sauce the sausages should simmer gently, not boil

content.text: Sausage casserole is a winter weeknight supper that works equally for children and adults. It is the kind of dish that can be started when you get home and is ready in under an hour. The sauce improves overnight, making it a good candidate for batch cooking.
